memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_number

detaileddescription.para: Gets the buffer number

xrefdescription.para: The buffer number now is equal to the object id

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Integer *src_id)
memberdef.name: src2ns

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer ns_id, Integer line_start, Integer line_end, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(7)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_clear_highlight

detaileddescription.para: Clears highlights and virtual text from namespace and range of lines

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Namespace to clear, or -1 to clear all.
Start of range of lines to clear
End of range of lines to clear (exclusive) or -1 to clear to end of file.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer ns_id, String hl_group, Integer line, Integer col_start, Integer col_end, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(13)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_add_highlight

detaileddescription.para: Adds a highlight to buffer.

Namespaces are used for batch deletion/updating of a set of highlights. To create a namespace, use |nvim_create_namespace()| which returns a namespace id. Pass it in to this function as ns_id to add highlights to the namespace. All highlights in the same namespace can then be cleared with single call to |nvim_buf_clear_namespace()|. If the highlight never will be deleted by an API call, pass ns_id = -1.

As a shorthand, ns_id = 0 can be used to create a new namespace for the highlight, the allocated id is then returned. If hl_group is the empty string no highlight is added, but a new ns_id is still returned. This is supported for backwards compatibility, new code should use |nvim_create_namespace()| to create a new empty namespace.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
namespace to use or -1 for ungrouped highlight
Name of the highlight group to use
Start of (byte-indexed) column range to highlight
End of (byte-indexed) column range to highlight, or -1 to highlight to end of line

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer src_id, Integer line, Array chunks, Dict(empty) *opts, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(8)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_virtual_text

detaileddescription.para: Set the virtual text (annotation) for a buffer line.

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_set_extmark to use full virtual text functionality.

detaileddescription.para: The text will be placed after the buffer text. Virtual text will never cause reflow, rather virtual text will be truncated at the end of the screen line. The virtual text will begin one cell (|lcs-eol| or space) after the ordinary text.

Namespaces are used to support batch deletion/updating of virtual text. To create a namespace, use |nvim_create_namespace()|. Virtual text is cleared using |nvim_buf_clear_namespace()|. The same ns_id can be used for both virtual text and highlights added by |nvim_buf_add_highlight()|, both can then be cleared with a single call to |nvim_buf_clear_namespace()|. If the virtual text never will be cleared by an API call, pass ns_id = -1.
As a shorthand, ns_id = 0 can be used to create a new namespace for the virtual text, the allocated id is then returned.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
Namespace to use or 0 to create a namespace, or -1 for a ungrouped annotation
Line to annotate with virtual text (zero-indexed)
A list of [text, hl_group] arrays, each representing a text chunk with specified highlight. hl_group element can be omitted for no highlight.

memberdef.argsstring: (Integer hl_id, Boolean rgb, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(9)
memberdef.name: nvim_get_hl_by_id

detaileddescription.para: Gets a highlight definition by id. |hlID()|

memberdef.argsstring: (String name, Boolean rgb, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(9)
memberdef.name: nvim_get_hl_by_name

detaileddescription.para: Gets a highlight definition by name.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer lnum, ArrayOf(String) lines, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_insert

detaileddescription.para: Inserts a sequence of lines to a buffer at a certain index

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, lnum, lnum, true, lines)

parameterdescription.para: Insert the lines after lnum. If negative, appends to the end of the buffer.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer index, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_get_line

detaileddescription.para: Gets a buffer line

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_get_lines instead. for positive indices (including 0) use "nvim_buf_get_lines(buffer, index, index+1, true)" for negative indices use "nvim_buf_get_lines(buffer, index-1, index, true)"

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer index, String line, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_set_line

detaileddescription.para: Sets a buffer line

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_set_lines instead. for positive indices use "nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, index, index+1, true, [line])" for negative indices use "nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, index-1, index, true, [line])"

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer index, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_del_line

detaileddescription.para: Deletes a buffer line

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_set_lines instead. for positive indices use "nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, index, index+1, true, [])" for negative indices use "nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, index-1, index, true, [])"

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer start, Integer end, Boolean include_start, Boolean include_end, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_get_line_slice

detaileddescription.para: Retrieves a line range from the buffer

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_get_lines(buffer, newstart, newend, false) where newstart = start + int(not include_start) - int(start < 0) newend = end + int(include_end) - int(end < 0) int(bool) = 1 if bool is true else 0

parameterdescription.para: True if the slice includes the start parameter
True if the slice includes the end parameter

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, Integer start, Integer end, Boolean include_start, Boolean include_end, ArrayOf(String) replacement, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_set_line_slice

detaileddescription.para: Replaces a line range on the buffer

xrefdescription.para: use nvim_buf_set_lines(buffer, newstart, newend, false, lines) where newstart = start + int(not include_start) + int(start < 0) newend = end + int(include_end) + int(end < 0) int(bool) = 1 if bool is true else 0

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer
True if the slice includes the start parameter
True if the slice includes the end parameter
Array of lines to use as replacement (0-length

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Object value, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_set_var

detaileddescription.para: Sets a buffer-scoped (b:) variable

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

simplesect.para: Old value or nil if there was no previous value. @warning It may return nil if there was no previous value or if previous value was `v:null`.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: buffer_del_var

detaileddescription.para: Removes a buffer-scoped (b:) variable

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

detaileddescription.para: Sets a window-scoped (w:) variable

simplesect.para: Old value or nil if there was no previous value. @warning It may return nil if there was no previous value or if previous value was `v:null`.

memberdef.argsstring: (Window window, String name, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: window_del_var

detaileddescription.para: Removes a window-scoped (w:) variable

memberdef.argsstring: (Tabpage tabpage, String name, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: tabpage_del_var

memberdef.argsstring: (String name, Object value, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(1)
memberdef.name: vim_set_var

simplesect.para: May return nil if there was no previous value OR if previous value was v:null.
Old value or nil if there was no previous value.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the global value of an option.

memberdef.name: nvim_get_option

detaileddescription.para: Gets the global value of an option.

memberdef.argsstring: (Buffer buffer, String name, Error *err) FUNC_API_RET_ALLOC
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_get_option

detaileddescription.para: Gets a buffer option value

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Buffer buffer, String name, Object value, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(11)
memberdef.name: nvim_buf_set_option

detaileddescription.para: Sets a buffer option value. Passing nil as value deletes the option (only works if there's a global fallback)

parameterdescription.para: Buffer id, or 0 for current buffer

memberdef.argsstring: (Window window, String name, Error *err) FUNC_API_RET_ALLOC
memberdef.name: nvim_win_get_option

detaileddescription.para: Gets a window option value

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Window window, String name, Object value, Error *err) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(11)
memberdef.name: nvim_win_set_option

detaileddescription.para: Sets a window option value. Passing nil as value deletes the option (only works if there's a global fallback)

memberdef.argsstring: (void *from, OptScope scope, String name, Error *err)
memberdef.name: get_option_from

detaileddescription.para: Gets the value of a global or local (buffer, window) option.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to buffer or window for local option value.
Option scope. See OptScope in option.h.
Details of an error that may have occurred.

simplesect.para: the option value. Must be freed by caller.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, void *to, OptScope scope, String name, Object value, Error *err)
memberdef.name: set_option_to

detaileddescription.para: Sets the value of a global or local (buffer, window) option.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to buffer or window for local option value.
Option scope. See OptScope in option.h.
Details of an error that may have occurred.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, Array calls, Arena *arena, Error *err) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(12) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_call_atomic

detaileddescription.para: Calls many API methods atomically.

This has two main usages:
To perform several requests from an async context atomically, i.e. without interleaving redraws, RPC requests from other clients, or user interactions (however API methods may trigger autocommands or event processing which have such side effects, e.g. |:sleep| may wake timers).
To minimize RPC overhead (roundtrips) of a sequence of many requests.

parameterdescription.para: an array of calls, where each call is described by an array with two elements: the request name, and an array of arguments.
Validation error details (malformed calls parameter), if any. Errors from batched calls are given in the return value.

simplesect.para: Array of two elements. The first is an array of return values. The second is NIL if all calls succeeded. If a call resulted in an error, it is a three-element array with the zero-based index of the call which resulted in an error, the error type and the error message. If an error occurred, the values from all preceding calls will still be returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint64_t channel_id, String event) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(13) FUNC_API_REMOTE_ONLY
memberdef.name: nvim_subscribe

memberdef.name: nvim_unsubscribe

memberdef.argsstring: (String message, bool to_err, bool writeln)
memberdef.name: write_msg

detaileddescription.para: Writes a message to vim output or error buffer. The string is split and flushed after each newline. Incomplete lines are kept for writing later.

parameterdescription.para: true: message is an error (uses emsg instead of msg)

memberdef.argsstring: (String str) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(13)

memberdef.argsstring: (String str) FUNC_API_SINCE(1) FUNC_API_DEPRECATED_SINCE(13)
memberdef.name: nvim_err_writeln

memberdef.name: nvim_notify

detaileddescription.para: Use nvim_echo or nvim_exec_lua("vim.notify(...)", ...) instead.

parameterdescription.para: Message to display to the user
